Bowery Boys.description: The Bowery Boys were a nativist, anti-Catholic, and anti-Irish criminal gang based in the Bowery neighborhood of Manhattan, New York City, active from the 1830s to the 1860s. Founded by Mike Walsh and William Poole, the gang consisted of volunteer firemen and engaged in street fighting, knife fighting, assault, murder, robbery, arson, and rioting. They feuded with rival gangs such as the Dead Rabbits and were known for their distinctive uniform of a stovepipe hat, red shirt, and dark trousers tucked into boots.

descriptions: The Atlantic Guards were longtime allies of the Bowery Boys.
edges.evidence_quote: A longtime ally of the Bowery Boys

descriptions: The Bowery Boys were allied with the American Guards.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: American Guards, Atlantic Guards, Empire Guards, O'Connell Guards, True Blue Americans, American Republican Party (American Nativist Party, American Party), Order of the Star Spangled Banner (Anti-immigrant secret society)

Muerte a Secuestradores.description: Colombian paramilitary group and private army formed in 1981 by drug cartels, politicians, and landowners to protect economic interests and fight kidnapping. It assassinated political opponents and community organizers, and waged counterinsurgency against guerrilla groups like FARC-EP and M-19. Supported by the Medellín Cartel and U.S. corporations, it was active through the 1990s.

descriptions: M-19's kidnapping led to the creation of MAS by the Medellín Cartel, indicating conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: The kidnapping led to the creation of the MAS/ Muerte a Secuestradores ("Death to Kidnappers") paramilitary group by the Medellin cartel.

descriptions: Cali Cartel co-founded MAS with Medellín Cartel.
edges.evidence_quote: the Medellín and Cali cartels, as well as associated traffickers, formed the group Muerte a Secuestradores (MAS; "Death to Kidnappers").

descriptions: Cali Cartel was a founding member of MAS.
edges.evidence_quote: members of the Medellín Cartel, Cali Cartel, the Colombian military, the U.S.-based corporation Texas Petroleum, the Colombian legislature, small industrialists, and wealthy cattle ranchers came together in a series of meetings in Puerto Boyacá and formed a paramilitary organization known as Muerte a Secuestradores

descriptions: Muerte a Secuestradores is listed as an ally of Medellín Cartel in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Muerte a Secuestradores , Guadalajara Cartel (defunct) , The Extraditables (defunct) , La Corporación (defunct) , Oficina de Envigado , Los Priscos (defunct) , Gallón Gang (defunct) , Galician clans , Gulf Cartel , Nicaragua (alleged) , Cuba (alleged) , Panama (alleged, under Manuel Noriega )

descriptions: Medellín Cartel co-founded MAS with Cali Cartel.
edges.evidence_quote: The two cartels participated in other joint ventures in later years, such as the founding of Muerte a Secuestradores (MAS), who successfully returned Ochoa's kidnapped sister, Marta Nieves Ochoa.

descriptions: Medellín Cartel was a founding member of MAS.
edges.evidence_quote: members of the Medellín Cartel, Cali Cartel, the Colombian military, the U.S.-based corporation Texas Petroleum, the Colombian legislature, small industrialists, and wealthy cattle ranchers came together in a series of meetings in Puerto Boyacá and formed a paramilitary organization known as Muerte a Secuestradores

descriptions: Cartel de Medellín created the MAS group.
edges.evidence_quote: el Cartel de Medellín crea un grupo llamado Muerte A Secuestradores (MAS) con apoyo de todos los miembros incluido Pablo Escobar .

descriptions: The Medellín Cartel created the MAS as its private army.
edges.evidence_quote: De esta manera nació el grupo llamado Muerte a Secuestradores (MAS), un ejército privado de más de 2.230 hombres.

descriptions: MAS waged counterinsurgency warfare against M-19.
edges.evidence_quote: Muerte a Secuestradores assassinated political opponents and community organizers, and waged counterinsurgency warfare against guerrilla movements such as the FARC-EP and the M-19 .

edges.target: Asociación Campesina de Ganaderos y Agricultores del Magdalena Medio
descriptions: ACDEGAM provided legal front and logistics for MAS, and MAS backed up ACDEGAM's threats with violence.
edges.evidence_quote: ACDEGAM worked to promote anti-labor policies, and threatened anyone involved with organizing for labor or peasants' rights. The threats were backed up by the MAS, which would come in and attack or assassinate anyone who was suspected of being a 'subversive'.

edges.target: Asociación Campesina de Ganaderos y Agricultores del Magdalena Medio
descriptions: MAS backed ACDEGAM's threats with violence.

edges.target: Asociación Campesina de Ganaderos y Agricultores del Magdalena Medio
descriptions: ACDEGAM was created as a legal front for MAS and other paramilitary groups.
edges.evidence_quote: the Asociación Campesina de Ganaderos y Agricultores del Magdalena Medio ("Association of Middle Magdalena Ranchers and Farmers", ACDEGAM) was created to handle both the logistics and the public relations of the organization, and to provide a legal front for various paramilitary groups. The threats were backed up by the MAS

descriptions: Medellín Cartel members co-founded MAS.
edges.evidence_quote: members of the Medellín Cartel , the Colombian military, the U.S.-based corporation Texas Petroleum , the Colombian legislature, small industrialists, and wealthy cattle ranchers came together in a series of meetings in Puerto Boyacá , and formed a paramilitary organization known as Muerte a Secuestradores

descriptions: Medellín Cartel members were part of forming MAS.
edges.evidence_quote: Between the end of 1981 and the beginning of 1982, members of the Medellín Cartel, the Colombian military, ... formed a paramilitary organization known as Muerte a Secuestradores

descriptions: MAS collaborated with Cali Cartel and Medellín Cartel dissidents to form Los Pepes.
edges.evidence_quote: the Calí Cartel, dissidents within the Medellín Cartel, and the MAS worked together to create a new paramilitary organization known as Perseguidos Por Pablo Escobar

descriptions: MAS was formed to fight against guerrillas including FARC.
edges.evidence_quote: They formed this organization to defend their economic interests, to fight against the guerrillas

Wilmas 13.description: Wilmas 13 is a Mexican American street gang based in Wilmington, Los Angeles, California, controlled by the Mexican Mafia. It has four sub-gangs and is involved in drug trafficking, arms trafficking, murder, and extortion. The gang has been subject to federal racketeering indictments and civil injunctions. Its leadership includes members of the Mexican Mafia operating from California state prisons.

descriptions: Investigation into Westside Wilmas led to arrests of Mexican Mafia members.
edges.evidence_quote: A joint investigation by the Los Angeles Police Department (LAPD) and the Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI) into the Westside Wilmas gang in the Los Angeles Harbor Region commenced in October 2022 and culminated with the arrests of 12 Mexican Mafia members

descriptions: Wilmas 13 is controlled by the Mexican Mafia.
edges.evidence_quote: Wilmas 13 (W13) is a Mexican American gang from Wilmington, Los Angeles , California that is controlled by the Mexican Mafia.

descriptions: Top-level members of IMG Insane MEXICAN Gang report to the Mexican Mafia.
edges.evidence_quote: whose low-level members are rivals, top-level members report to the Mexican Mafia

State Line Mob.description: The State Line Mob was an association of criminal elements operating in the 1950s and 1960s along the Mississippi–Tennessee state line. Involved in bootlegging, gambling, prostitution, robbery, and murder, it owned motels, restaurants, and clubs that served as centers for vice. The group gained national attention for its feud with Sheriff Buford Pusser, which inspired the film Walking Tall.

edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Native Gangster Disciples , Folk Nation , Gangster Disciples , Native Disciples , Project Boyz , Moe Mob

descriptions: Haouchar crime network and Comanchero had a dispute over a drug debt.

St. Louis crime family.description: The St. Louis crime family was an Italian-American Mafia crime family based in St. Louis, Missouri, founded around the 1890s. It was involved in racketeering, gambling, drug trafficking, and other illegal activities. The family was active until approximately 2014, with ties to other Mafia families such as the Chicago Outfit and Detroit Partnership.

'Ndrina Iannazzo.description: The 'Ndrina Iannazzo is a 'ndrina (organized crime family) of the 'Ndrangheta in Calabria, based in Lamezia Terme. It is allied with the Giampà and Cannizzaro-Daponte clans. The group has been involved in a long-running feud with the Torcasio-Gualtieri-Cerra clan, resulting in dozens of murders. It has also infiltrated public contracts, including the modernization of the A3 motorway, and has been linked to political corruption. In 2022, assets worth €800 million were seized from its associates.

descriptions: Cerra-Torcasio-Gualtieri and Iannazzo-Giampà were in conflict in the Lamezia Terme feud.
edges.evidence_quote: Faida di Lamezia Terme : prese il via nel 2000 e si protrae a fasi alterne fino al 2011, cosche protagoniste sono i Cerra-Torcasio-Gualtieri e gli Iannazzo - Giampà

descriptions: Iannazzo is listed as an ally of Giampà in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Alleati: Dattilo, Iannazzo

descriptions: Giampà is mentioned alongside Iannazzo-Daponte-Cannizzaro in the same operation.
edges.evidence_quote: operazione Quinta Bolgia della DDA che porta a 24 ordinanze di custodia cautelare per la cosca degli Iannazzo-Daponte-Cannizzaro e Giampà

descriptions: Iannazzo is a 'ndrina of the 'Ndrangheta.
edges.evidence_quote: La 'ndrina Iannazzo è una cosca malavitosa o 'ndrina della 'ndrangheta calabrese

descriptions: Iannazzo and Cannizzaro-Daponte jointly carried out attacks against Torcasio.
edges.evidence_quote: viene ricostruito l'omicidio del presunto boss Antonio Torcasio del 23 maggio 2003 e il tentato omicidio contro Vincenzo Torcasio ad opera degli Iannazzo e dei Cannizzaro-Daponte.

descriptions: Iannazzo is allied with Cannizzaro-Daponte.
edges.evidence_quote: alleata dei Giampà e Cannizzaro-Daponte

descriptions: Iannazzo and Cannizzaro-Daponte jointly attempted a murder.
edges.evidence_quote: il tentato omicidio contro Vincenzo Torcasio ad opera degli Iannazzo e dei Cannizzaro-Daponte

descriptions: Iannazzo is in a faida (conflict) with Torcasio-Gualtieri-Cerra.
edges.evidence_quote: faida ... tra i Torcasio-Gualtieri-Cerra e gli Iannazzo- Giampà

descriptions: Iannazzo (with Giampà) made peace with Torcasio-Gualtieri in 2015.
edges.evidence_quote: Nel 2015 con l'operazione Andromeda viene fatta luce sulla pace tra gli Iannazzo-Giampà ed i Torcasio-Gualtieri avvenuta anche grazie alla mediazione di clan del reggino.

descriptions: Dattilo is listed as an ally of Iannazzo.
edges.evidence_quote: Alleati: ... Dattilo

descriptions: De Luca is listed as an ally of Iannazzo.
edges.evidence_quote: Alleati: ... De Luca

descriptions: Iannazzo-Daponte-Cannizzaro and Giampà are mentioned together as part of the same criminal network.
edges.evidence_quote: Il 12 novembre 2018 si conclude l'operazione Quinta Bolgia ... per la cosca degli Iannazzo-Daponte-Cannizzaro e Giampà che fanno riferimento a Vincenzo Torcasio

descriptions: Iannazzo is allied with Giampà.
edges.evidence_quote: alleata dei Giampà e Cannizzaro-Daponte

descriptions: Iannazzo and Giampà are allied against Torcasio-Gualtieri-Cerra.
edges.evidence_quote: tra i Torcasio-Gualtieri-Cerra e gli Iannazzo - Giampà

descriptions: Pagliuso is listed as an ally of Iannazzo.
edges.evidence_quote: Alleati: ... Pagliuso

descriptions: Torcasio is in a feud with Iannazzo.
edges.evidence_quote: Nel 2000 scoppia una faida ( faida di Lamezia Terme ) che si trascina ancora oggi tra i Torcasio-Gualtieri-Cerra e gli Iannazzo - Giampà

descriptions: Torcasio made peace with Iannazzo in 2015.
edges.evidence_quote: Viene fatta luce anche sulla pace tra gli Iannazzo - Giampà ed i Torcasio-Gualtieri
